items.name: SMART Goals
items.best_for: Clear, concrete goals with obvious success criteria
items.pros: Easy to understand, forces specificity, widely known
items.cons: Can feel rigid, doesn't capture ambitious stretch goals well
items.example: Exercise: 'Run 3x/week for 30 min, Mon/Wed/Fri, tracked in Strava, for 8 weeks'

items.name: OKRs (Objectives & Key Results)
items.best_for: Ambitious goals that need measurable milestones
items.pros: Separates vision from metrics, encourages stretch goals, standard scoring
items.cons: More complex to set up, requires quarterly review discipline
items.example: Objective: Become confident system designer. KR1: Complete 15 practice problems. KR2: Read 3 architecture books.

items.name: Habit Stacking (Atomic Habits)
items.description: Link new habits to existing routines using 'After I X, I will Y'
items.pros: Leverages existing neural pathways, requires no motivation, automatic
items.cons: Takes time to establish, doesn't work for one-time goals
items.example: After I pour coffee, I will review today's 3 priorities.

items.name: 1-3-5 Rule
items.description: 1 big goal, 3 medium goals, 5 small goals — prevents overload
items.best_for: Prioritizing when you have many goals competing for attention
items.pros: Forces prioritization, prevents goal overload, clear hierarchy
items.cons: Can feel limiting if you have genuinely important goals in all areas
items.example: Big: Get promoted. Medium: Learn system design, lead 2 projects, mentor 1 junior. Small: Weekly tasks.

items.name: WOOP (Wish, Outcome, Obstacle, Plan)
items.description: Identify your wish, best outcome, worst obstacles, and if-then plans
items.best_for: Goals where you know the obstacles are significant
items.pros: Mental contrasting increases follow-through, proactive obstacle planning
items.cons: Can feel pessimistic, requires honest obstacle assessment
items.example: Wish: Learn Rust. Outcome: Build CLI tool. Obstacle: Time pressure. Plan: If tired after work, study 15 min instead of skipping.

comparisons.insight: These frameworks aren't mutually exclusive. Combine them: use SMART for clarity, OKRs for ambition, Habit Stacking for execution, and 1-3-5 for prioritization. Pick the 1-2 that resonate and use them consistently.

keyInsight: The best goal framework is the one you'll actually use. Start with SMART goals for clarity, add OKRs for ambition, and use Habit Stacking for daily execution. Review and adjust quarterly — goals are tools, not sacred vows.
